questions.question: Which among the following is the SI physical unit of dynamic viscosity?
questions.explanation: The SI unit for dynamic viscosity η is the Pascal-second (Pa-s). It corresponds to the force (N) per unit area (m2) divided by the rate of shear (s-1).

questions.question: What is the SI unit of viscosity?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of viscosity is poiseiulle (Pl). Its other units are N s m-2 or Pa s.

questions.question: Which of these fluids has the highest viscosity?
questions.explanation: Viscosities of some common fluids: Water:1 Honey: 200 Blood: 2.7 Air: 0.017

questions.question: What happens to the viscosity of a liquid with increase in temperature?
questions.explanation: The viscosity of liquids decreases with increase in temperature, while it increases in the case of gases.

questions.question: What do we call the maximum velocity of a fluid in a tube for which the flow remains streamlined?
questions.explanation: The maximum velocity of a fluid in a tube for which the flow remains streamlined is called its critical velocity.
